root_cause: HPA 'checkout-api-hpa' cannot compute desired replicas because autoscaling metrics are unavailable
evidence: Recent horizontal-pod-autoscaler warnings repeated 3 times in the last 20 minutes
Latest HPA metrics warning: invalid metrics (1 invalid out of 1), first error is: failed to get cpu resource metric value: no metrics returned from resource metrics API

causes.message: HorizontalPodAutoscaler cannot calculate desired replicas because required metrics are unavailable or invalid
Scale decisions for the workload are stalled until metric collection or adapter access recovers

hpa:checkout-api-hpa: the HPA was unable to compute the replica count: unable to get metrics for resource cpu: no metrics returned from resource metrics API
invalid metrics (1 invalid out of 1), first error is: failed to get cpu resource metric value: no metrics returned from resource metrics API
pod:checkout-api-7f6c7c89f6-r9l4m: Pod belongs to autoscaled workload Deployment/checkout-api

likely_causes: Metrics Server or a custom/external metrics adapter is unavailable or returning errors
The HPA metric spec no longer matches a serving metric series or selector
One or more autoscaled containers are missing the resource requests required for utilization-based metrics
API aggregation or control-plane connectivity problems are preventing the HPA controller from reading metrics
suggested_checks: kubectl get hpa checkout-api-hpa -n payments -o yaml
kubectl get --raw /apis/metrics.k8s.io/v1beta1/  # or inspect the custom/external metrics adapter
Verify every autoscaled container defines the CPU/memory requests required by the HPA metric type
